hypothesis: Incorporating explicit uncertainty quantification into the physics-based synthesis simulator (treating CALPHAD equilibria and kinetic parameters as probability distributions) and pairing it with an uncertainty-aware prompting strategy for the LLM will yield more robust, data-sparse multi-component oxide synthesis plans with higher success rates and lower plan variance than a deterministic LLM planner, as demonstrated on Nb–O–Fe or Nb–O–Sb ternary systems.
